scenario: Meridian Insurance is building a custom outbound connector with the Camunda 8.8 Connector SDK to score 60000 transactions per day against a proprietary fraud API. The Java class annotated @OutboundConnector exposes the name MyFraudConnector with type io.camunda:fraud-connector:1 and must be reachable by the connector runtime as a job worker within 5 seconds of deployment.
question: Which mechanism connects the BPMN task to this Java implementation at runtime?

options.text: The element template declares a hidden property bound to zeebe:taskDefinition with the property type set to the same string io.camunda:fraud-connector:1 so the job type matches
The connector runtime scans the BPMN XML for the literal class name MyFraudConnector and instantiates it directly through Java reflection at deploy time
The element template stores the fully qualified Java class name in a zeebe:taskHeader so the runtime can load it from the classpath when the job activates
The connector runtime registers a single global job type for every custom connector and dispatches by inspecting the input variables on each activated job

a.text: Correct. The element template binds zeebe:taskDefinition.type to the value declared in @OutboundConnector, which is the job type the connector worker fetches.

b.text: Incorrect. The runtime does not scan the BPMN XML for Java class names; the binding is via the job type registered in zeebe:taskDefinition.

c.text: Incorrect. Job headers carry per-task configuration such as resultVariable or errorExpression, not the connector implementation class to load.

d.text: Incorrect. Each custom connector registers its own job type; there is no single global type that dispatches by input shape across all connectors.

explanation: An outbound connector's @OutboundConnector type matches the zeebe:taskDefinition type value declared in the element template. The connector runtime registers a job worker for that exact type to fetch and execute jobs.

docs.title: Connector SDK - outbound connector element template
